acceptance_criteria:
- [ ] IpcError defined in beads-surface
- [ ] encode/decode/framing functions in beads-surface
- [ ] No impl From<DaemonError> for ErrorPayload (orphan rule)
- [ ] All 161 error conversions use explicit .to_error_payload()
- [ ] `cargo check -p beads-surface` passes
- [ ] `cargo check -p beads-rs` passes
description:
**Problem**
`daemon/ipc/codec.rs` does two things:
1. NDJSON framing + JSON encode/decode (belongs in surface)
2. Mapping daemon errors to ErrorPayload (belongs in daemon)

**Orphan rule issue**:
Currently `impl From<OpError> for ErrorPayload` exists. After extraction, ErrorPayload is in beads-core (foreign), OpError is in beads-rs, so this `From` impl becomes illegal.

**Affected impls** (daemon/ipc/codec.rs):
- Line 23: `impl From<OpError> for ErrorPayload`
- Line 879: `impl From<IpcError> for ErrorPayload`

**Call sites**: 161 uses of `Response::err(e)` where `e: OpError` relies on implicit conversion.

**Recommended fix** (minimal changes):
1. Add `IntoErrorPayload` trait in beads-rs:
   ```rust
   pub trait IntoErrorPayload {
       fn into_error_payload(self) -> ErrorPayload;
   }
   impl IntoErrorPayload for OpError { ... }
   ```
2. Add extension method on Response in beads-rs:
   ```rust
   pub trait ResponseExt {
       fn err_from<E: IntoErrorPayload>(e: E) -> Response;
   }
   ```
3. Find-replace: `Response::err(` → `Response::err_from(` in daemon code

This keeps surface clean (no `impl Into` bounds) and changes are mechanical.

**Changes**

1. **Move framing code to beads-surface/src/ipc/codec.rs**:
   - `IpcError` (parse/io/frame-too-large/version mismatch)
   - `encode_response`, `send_response`, `decode_request_with_limits`, `read_requests`

2. **Create daemon error mapping** `crates/beads-rs/src/daemon/ipc/error_mapping.rs`:
   - `IntoErrorPayload` trait
   - `impl IntoErrorPayload for OpError`
   - `impl IntoErrorPayload for IpcError`
   - `ResponseExt` trait for `Response::err_from()`

3. **Mechanical find-replace** in daemon code:
   `Response::err(` → `Response::err_from(`

**Files**
- crates/beads-surface/src/ipc/codec.rs (framing only)
- crates/beads-rs/src/daemon/ipc/error_mapping.rs (new)
- crates/beads-rs/src/daemon/ipc/mod.rs
- Daemon files (mechanical find-replace)
title: Phase 3E: Refactor IPC codec into surface + daemon error mapping

description:
**Problem**
The Request enum in src/daemon/ipc/types.rs has 11 admin-related variants at the top level:
- AdminStatus
- AdminMetrics  
- AdminDoctor
- AdminScrub
- AdminFlush
- AdminCheckpointWait
- AdminFingerprint
- AdminReloadPolicies
- AdminReloadLimits
- AdminReloadReplication
- AdminRotateReplicaId
- AdminMaintenanceMode
- AdminRebuildIndex

This adds cognitive load when pattern matching (47 total variants) and makes the Request enum harder to navigate. Admin operations are a cohesive group that should be nested.

**Design**
1. Create `AdminOp` enum with all admin variants (without Admin prefix):

```rust
// src/daemon/ipc/types.rs

#[derive(Debug, Clone, Serialize, Deserialize)]
#[serde(tag = "admin_op", rename_all = "snake_case")]
pub enum AdminOp {
    Status { repo: PathBuf },
    Metrics { repo: PathBuf },
    Doctor { repo: PathBuf, fix: bool },
    Scrub { repo: PathBuf, fix: bool },
    Flush { repo: PathBuf },
    CheckpointWait { repo: PathBuf, timeout_ms: Option<u64> },
    Fingerprint { repo: PathBuf, mode: AdminFingerprintMode, sample: AdminFingerprintSample },
    ReloadPolicies { repo: PathBuf },
    ReloadLimits { repo: PathBuf },
    ReloadReplication { repo: PathBuf },
    RotateReplicaId { repo: PathBuf },
    MaintenanceMode { repo: PathBuf, enabled: bool },
    RebuildIndex { repo: PathBuf },
}
```

2. Replace 11 Request variants with one:

```rust
#[derive(Debug, Clone, Serialize, Deserialize)]
#[serde(tag = "op", rename_all = "snake_case")]
pub enum Request {
    // ... other variants
    Admin(AdminOp),  // Replaces 11 AdminX variants
}
```

3. Update dispatch in coord.rs:

```rust
Request::Admin(op) => self.handle_admin(op),
```

4. Update CLI admin.rs handler to construct AdminOp variants

**Design Notes**
- Wire format changes: `{"op": "admin_status", ...}` → `{"op": "admin", "admin_op": "status", ...}`
- This is a breaking protocol change — requires IPC_PROTOCOL_VERSION bump to 3
- Can be done after IPC documentation bead to have clear migration notes

**Acceptance**
- [ ] AdminOp enum created with all 11 variants
- [ ] Request enum has single Admin(AdminOp) variant
- [ ] IPC_PROTOCOL_VERSION bumped to 3
- [ ] coord.rs dispatch updated
- [ ] src/cli/commands/admin.rs updated to construct AdminOp
- [ ] Wire format documented in IPC_PROTOCOL.md
- [ ] cargo clippy passes
- [ ] cargo test passes
- [ ] Integration tests pass

**Files:** src/daemon/ipc/types.rs, src/daemon/coord.rs, src/cli/commands/admin.rs, docs/IPC_PROTOCOL.md
title: Nest admin Request variants under Request::Admin(AdminOp)

description:
## What's Wrong
Bead IDs now support `<slug>-<suffix>` to match beads-go (repo-name slug), but new repos still default to `bd` because slug selection is inferred from existing state.

## Where
- src/daemon/executor.rs: preferred_bead_slug(...) falls back to "bd" when store is empty
- src/git/wire.rs: meta.json only stores format_version; no place to persist root slug

## Why It Matters
- Fresh repos should default to the repo-name slug (beads-go parity) or at least allow configuring it.
- Inferring slug from existing IDs fails for empty stores and is an implicit heuristic.

## Suggested Fix
- Add `root_slug` to `meta.json` (WireMeta + core Meta), and set it on init (derive from repo dir name or remote URL basename; sanitize).
- Use persisted `root_slug` for new ID generation.
- Decide compatibility strategy (format_version bump if needed; ensure old readers ignore unknown fields or gate by version).

## Acceptance
- After `bd init` in a repo named `foo`, new IDs default to `foo-...` (or a clearly documented rule).
- `bd create` in an empty store uses `meta.root_slug`, not a heuristic.
title: Persist root slug in meta.json; default new IDs to repo-name

description: src/cli/commands/update.rs fetches issue after updates just to print updated id. For non-JSON output, avoid fetch and print the id from args; keep fetch only for --json.
title: CLI update: skip fetch for non-JSON output

description:
**Problem**
`Daemon::apply_update` applies `BeadPatch` field-by-field inline in `src/daemon/executor.rs`. The patch semantics live in `src/daemon/ops.rs`, but the actual application logic lives elsewhere, so adding or changing fields requires touching multiple places and is easy to miss.

**Design**
Add a helper on `BeadPatch` to apply patches directly to `BeadFields`:
- Implement `BeadPatch::apply_to_fields(&self, fields: &mut BeadFields, stamp: &Stamp) -> Result<(), OpError>` in `src/daemon/ops.rs`.
- Move the current field-application logic (including label parsing and status -> workflow transition) into this helper.
- Update `Daemon::apply_update` to call the helper inside the WAL mutation.

**Design Notes**
Keep behavior identical: required fields cannot be cleared, label parsing stays strict, and status strings map to the same workflows with the same validation errors.

**Acceptance**
- [ ] `Daemon::apply_update` delegates patch application to `BeadPatch::apply_to_fields`.
- [ ] Label validation and workflow transitions behave exactly as before.
- [ ] Run `codex review --uncommitted` (timeout 10m) and address findings.
- [ ] Tests pass.

**Files:** `src/daemon/ops.rs`, `src/daemon/executor.rs`
title: Move BeadPatch application into BeadPatch helper

description: Multiple commands call send() repeatedly (show/update/label/delete/create --file), each opening a Unix socket and version ping. Add a small client wrapper to reuse one connection per command and verify version once.
title: CLI IPC: reuse daemon connection per command

acceptance_criteria:
- [ ] `beads-git` test-only path override support is not part of the normal production-facing public API surface
- [ ] daemon-owned tests still have a clean way to isolate git-owned checkpoint cache paths
- [ ] existing checkpoint isolation coverage continues to pass
description:
**Problem**
The checkpoint-cache isolation fix for `beads-rs-gou3.8.2` exposes `#[doc(hidden)]` test-only data-dir override helpers from `beads-git` so daemon-owned tests can isolate git cache paths after the crate split. This is acceptable for now and currently only consumed by `crates/beads-daemon/src/paths.rs`, but it does widen the public surface of a leaf crate for test support.

**Why It Matters**
The main crate-boundary epic is still ready to close because the runtime/public surfaces are sealed and all gates pass, but this is worth tightening later so test-only plumbing stays out of the normal production API.

**Files**
- `crates/beads-git/src/lib.rs`
- `crates/beads-git/src/paths.rs`
- `crates/beads-daemon/src/paths.rs`
title: Tighten beads-git test-only data-dir override surface after checkpoint-cache isolation fix
